title: Model Lab Heatmap View, Progressive Parcel Detail, Right-Panel Simplification, and Performance Polish

heatmap_aggregation_redesign.countywide_display: Aggregated countywide research surface using capped grid cells instead of drawing the full parcel point cloud.
heatmap_aggregation_redesign.medium_zoom_display: Clustered research markers from aggregated safe preview records.
heatmap_aggregation_redesign.parcel_scale_display: Capped parcel-scale research markers for closer inspection.

right_panel_simplification.default_state: Model Lab Intelligence now emphasizes current map display, visible safe research context, dominant signal, preview row count, aggregate model status, and key aggregate metrics.
right_panel_simplification.selected_state: Selected Model Research Context shows safe signal band, relative rank band, heatmap/cluster/parcel context, represented record count, top drivers, why highlighted, and caveats.
removed_clutter: future model placeholder list in the operational panel

explains: Why probability values are not shown

left_panel_model_lab_controls.summary: Model Lab controls are shorter and operational: model status, overlay toggle, Auto by zoom display mode, current map mode, compact legend, feature groups used/excluded, Save Snapshot, and Methodology link.
left_panel_model_lab_controls.advanced_layers: Generic map layers remain available inside a collapsed Advanced map layers section.

snapshot_report_integration.executive_report: Executive Summary includes Development Model Research Context for Model Lab snapshots, including map display, selected heatmap cell/cluster/parcel context, drivers, missing data, and the internal-only caveat.

performance_improvements: Avoids rendering all parcel markers at county extent.
Aggregates visible preview records into grid cells at broad and medium zoom.
Caps rendered graphics by display mode.
Publishes lightweight map summary state for the right panel instead of recomputing panel context from every marker.
Keeps right-panel state changes separate from overlay source data.
documentation_updates: README Model Lab section now describes countywide surface, clusters, parcel markers, and metric drawer.
Nontechnical model explanation now explains progressive map display.
Expected Q&A now explains the Development Research Signal as relative rank context.
Model readiness doc now includes Phase 23D progressive map display governance notes.

safety_verification.notes: Browser smoke and pytest guard checked relevant UI strings for unsafe probability/raw-score/ranking-class exposure.

validation_results.dev_server: CFS was already running on localhost:3000 and 127.0.0.1:8000, so smoke testing reused the active CFS dev stack instead of launching a second npm run dev:cfs process.

smoke_test_results.research_overlay_on_countywide_display: passed: right panel reported Countywide research surface with safe represented-record count.
smoke_test_results.selected_research_context: passed: map click selected a countywide heatmap cell with relative band, represented count, drivers, and caveat.
smoke_test_results.executive_summary_model_section: passed: report included map display, selected heatmap cell context, drivers, missing data, and internal-only caveat.
smoke_test_results.browser_console: Known opaque console error 'r' observed; no new actionable console error identified.
smoke_test_results.zoom_transition_browser_check: inconclusive in automated browser because SceneView scroll/zoom did not yield a stable visible display-mode update, but source thresholds and countywide display were verified.

remaining_limitations: Automated browser zoom-transition verification was inconclusive; manual QA should zoom from countywide to parcel scale to visually confirm clustered and parcel-detail transitions.
Model Lab remains internal research only and should not be presented as a public prediction product.
Map screenshot capture still depends on ArcGIS SceneView screenshot availability.
The known opaque browser console error 'r' remains from prior smoke tests.
